- `POST /auth/reset/<token>` → verify token, update `password_hash`, mark token used, invalidate all of that user's sessions (force re-login), invalidate all other reset tokens for the user.

Rate limits: per-email (5/hour) and per-IP (20/hour). Both use the existing in-memory deque pattern from login/signup.

### C. /settings additions

- **Change password** — requires current password. Invalidates other sessions.
- **Change email** — starts verification flow (section A).
- **Linked accounts** — show Google linkage. "Link Google Account" (starts OAuth with `state=link&user_id=<me>`; callback branches on state). "Unlink Google" (only allowed if a password is set, else user would be locked out).
- **Delete account** — requires password or Google re-auth, soft-delete with 30-day grace (new `deleted_at` column, nightly hard-delete cron).

### D. Account merging

Two cases:
1. **Have Google-only account, want password**: not a merge — just "set password" in /settings.
2. **Two separate accounts exist (one password, one Google, different emails)**: explicit merge while signed in.

Proposed merge flow (manual, user-initiated):
- Signed in as account A. "Merge another account into this one" in /settings.
- Authenticate as account B (password form or Google sign-in with `state=merge_source&into=A`).
- Show confirmation: "Everything owned by @B will move to @A. @B will be deleted. This cannot be undone."
- On confirm: reassign `owner_id` on wikis, api_keys, stars, forks, audit_log rows; rename git repos (`repos/B/foo.git` → `repos/A/foo.git`, respecting slug collisions by appending `-b` suffix); delete B.

Risks to name explicitly in ticket:
- Slug collisions (A and B both have a wiki slug `notes`) — we have to rename one.
- Subdomain collisions (wikis have globally-unique subdomains) — likely already safe since B's subdomain is distinct, but verify.
- External references (published URLs like `https://B.wikihub.md` → break unless we 301 to `A.wikihub.md`).
- Git clone URLs in users' local repos stop working after merge.

Given that risk surface, propose we ship "set password on Google-only account" (case 1) now, and defer the cross-account merge (case 2) until someone actually asks for it. It's a lot of code for a rare need.

### E. Fixing the Google auto-link hole

In `google_callback`:
```python
user = User.query.filter_by(google_id=google_id).first()
if not user and email and email_verified_from_google:
    candidate = User.query.filter_by(email=email).first()
    if candidate and candidate.email_verified_at is not None:
        candidate.google_id = google_id
        user = candidate
    # else: fall through to "create new account" below
```

Only link if both sides verified the email. This should ship alongside the email-verification column so we don't retro-link existing unverified matches.

## Recommended shipping order

Each is a beads subtask under wikihub-ks5t.

1. Schema migration: add `email_verified_at`, swap to partial unique index. Backfill `email_verified_at=NULL` for everyone (no one is verified today).
2. Email-sending pipeline (pick provider — Postmark? Resend? AWS SES? — smallest footprint). Single module with `send_transactional(to, subject, body)`.
3. Email verification flow + banner in header when unverified. On signup, send verification. On Google sign-in, mark verified if Google says so.
4. Fix Google auto-link (section E). Requires #1 and #3.
5. Password reset flow (`/auth/forgot`, `/auth/reset/<token>`). Requires #2 and #3.
6. /settings: change password, change email, linked accounts.
7. Delete account (soft + cron).
8. (Maybe later) cross-account merge.

## Open questions

- Email provider: Postmark is cheapest-to-start and has good deliverability, but costs ~$15/mo. AWS SES is ~free but needs reputation warm-up + bounce handling. Resend is developer-friendly. Leaning Postmark for MVP. **Ask Jacob.**
- Should unverified-email signups get full access, or gate wiki creation on verification? Leaning full access with a banner — friction at signup loses users. Gate only "destructive" or "public" actions (publishing a wiki to subdomain?) if needed later.
- Session-invalidation on password change — we don't currently track sessions in DB; Flask-Login cookies are signed tokens. To "invalidate all sessions" we'd need to bump a `session_version` on the user and check it on every request. Propose adding that column now.
- Delete account: also delete git repos or keep them? Keep for grace period, then purge. Need to document in privacy policy.

title: Schema: add email_verified_at + partial unique index on verified emails
description: Add `email_verified_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL` to users. Swap email uniqueness from simple UNIQUE to a PostgreSQL partial unique index: `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ux_users_email_verified ON users(email) WHERE email_verified_at IS NOT NULL`. Keeps unverified claims non-exclusive (first-to-verify wins), which unblocks (a) multiple users claiming the same unverified email and (b) the Google auto-link fix. Backfill: `email_verified_at = NULL` for all existing users (no one is verified today). Write an Alembic migration.

title: Email-sending pipeline: transactional send() module + provider decision
description: **Status: likely merge-or-depend with wikihub-exj1** — that ticket already scopes "email provider + basic send helper + env vars + templates" as infra for share notifications. This ticket's original scope was a generic app/email.py with send_transactional(). Proposal: let wikihub-exj1 own provider selection + base send helper; scope this ticket down to only the transactional-auth-specific templates (verify, reset, account-notification) once that infra lands. Alternatively, close this and fold its content into exj1's "Infra first" section. Decision needed before starting. See scratch/auth_design.md §B.

title: Email verification flow: signup sends verify link, banner until verified, Google emails auto-verified
description: Add `EmailVerificationToken(id, user_id, new_email, token_hash, expires_at, used_at)` with 24h TTL, single-use. Signup with email: create user with email set, email_verified_at=NULL, mint token, send link. /auth/verify/<token>: swap email on User, set email_verified_at=now(). Settings flow: 'Change email' mints a token on the new address; only swaps on click. Google OAuth: when Google's id_token has email_verified=true, set email_verified_at=now() at account creation. Add a dismissible 'verify your email' banner in base template when current user has an email but email_verified_at is null. Don't gate any actions on verification yet.

title: Fix Google OAuth auto-link-by-unverified-email takeover vector
description: Current google_callback links google_id to any user whose email matches, regardless of verification — allowing a pre-claimed unverified email to hijack a real Google account's identity mapping. Change: only auto-link when the matched user's email_verified_at IS NOT NULL AND Google's id_token reports email_verified=true. Otherwise, fall through to 'create new account'. Add a test that covers: (a) unverified email match → no link, new account, (b) verified email match → link OK. Depends on schema + verification flow being shipped first to avoid retro-linking.

title: Password reset flow: /auth/forgot and /auth/reset/<token>
description: Add `PasswordResetToken(id, user_id, token_hash, expires_at, used_at)`, 30-min TTL, single-use. POST /auth/forgot {email}: always returns 204 (no enumeration). If a verified email matches a user with password_hash set → mint token + email reset link. If matched user is Google-only → email says 'your account uses Google Sign-In, sign in with Google'. No match → silent no-op. GET /auth/reset/<token> → form. POST /auth/reset/<token> → verify token (unused + unexpired), update password_hash, mark token used, invalidate all other reset tokens for that user, bump session_version (see #6) to kick other sessions. Rate limit: 5/hr per email, 20/hr per IP (deque pattern like login). Requires email-sending + verification infra.

title: /settings: change password (with session invalidation), change email, linked accounts, link/unlink Google
description: Add to /settings: (1) Change password — requires current password, updates password_hash, bumps session_version on User (new column) to force re-login elsewhere (Flask-Login doesn't track sessions server-side, so we check session_version on each request). (2) Change email — kicks off verification flow on new address. (3) Linked accounts panel — shows Google linkage; 'Link Google Account' launches OAuth with state=link&user_id=me so callback adds google_id to current user instead of creating a new one; 'Unlink Google' allowed only if password_hash is set (else user would be locked out). Add session_version column to User; include in session payload; reject sessions whose version mismatches current.

title: Delete account: soft-delete with 30-day grace period + nightly hard-delete cron
description: Add `deleted_at TIMESTAMPTZ NULL` to User. Settings → 'Delete account' → re-auth (password or Google), confirm, set deleted_at=now(). Treat deleted users as logged-out everywhere; hide their wikis from listings/search (but keep data for 30d). Nightly cron (e.g. systemd timer or simple apscheduler job) hard-deletes users where deleted_at < now() - 30d: delete wikis, pages, git repos (both .git and -public.git), api_keys, stars, forks, audit_log, user row. Recovery UX (undo): 'your account is scheduled for deletion on Y; click to cancel' banner if signed in during grace period.

title: DEFERRED: cross-account merge (two existing accounts → one)
description: Out of scope for Google-Drive readiness milestone; capturing so it's not lost. Design sketch in scratch/auth_design.md §D case 2. Risks: wiki slug collisions, subdomain uniqueness, external URL breakage (https://B.wikihub.md → https://A.wikihub.md), broken git clones in users' checkouts. Don't implement until a real user asks. For now, the 'I have a Google-only account and want a password' case is handled by /settings → 'Set password' (part of #6).

title: Reconsider: WikiHub CLI (Obsidian shipped official CLI Feb 2026)
description:
Earlier conclusion (2026-04-20) was 'probably not worth building a WikiHub CLI right now' — partly based on the claim that Obsidian had no official CLI. That's wrong: Obsidian 1.12.0 shipped an official CLI in early access Feb 10, 2026, GA in v1.12.4 on Feb 27, 2026 (100+ commands).

This reopens the question. Strongest case for a WikiHub CLI:
- Agent-facing composable surface: pipe into jq, wire into shell scripts, cron publish
- Easier to install-and-script than requiring an MCP server
- Onboarding: wikihub signup && wikihub new && wikihub publish

Wraps existing REST/MCP layer; doesn't replace git auth flow.

Obsidian CLI refs:
- https://obsidian.md/cli
- https://help.obsidian.md/cli

Decide: build thin CLI, or hold and revisit if agent/scripter demand materializes.

title: draggable resize handle between content and right sidebar not implemented
description: the border between the article content and the right sidebar panel is not draggable. user wants to be able to resize the split. store width preference in localStorage. this was filed before as wikihub-9gm but confirming it's still needed.

title: Private-link permission screen: decide logged-out request-access CTA
description: Evaluate whether private-or-missing screens should offer a Request access action when the visitor is logged out. Recommendation should preserve the existing anti-enumeration model (same treatment for private and missing pages), define safe copy, decide whether wiki root and deep page URLs differ, and outline the minimal Flask implementation shape if adopted.

title: Auth page navigation: clickable logo → home + hover state
description:
All auth pages (login, signup, forgot-password, reset-password) now have the [[wikihub]] logo as a clickable link to /. Logo bracket color transitions to accent on hover. No full nav bar needed — focused auth card UX with logo as the escape hatch is the industry standard (GitHub, Slack, Linear pattern).

Shipped in this batch — just documenting for the record.

title: Editor autosave and unsaved-changes warning
description:
Google Drive readiness assessment found that the current editor is a textarea + preview + form submit, with no autosave or clear unsaved-changes protection.

Desired behavior:
- warn before navigation if content has unsaved changes
- optionally save draft state locally or autosave after a debounce
- preserve the current lightweight markdown editor; this is a UX-hardening ticket, not a WYSIWYG rewrite

Why:
This is a cheap but meaningful gap-closer versus Google Docs for normal teammates.

Evidence:
- /tmp/wikihub-gdrive-assessment.md
- app/templates/editor.html

title: Magic login links — exchange API key for one-time browser login URL
description:
Add endpoint that an agent (with API key) can call to generate a short-lived, one-time-use URL that logs the user into the browser without leaking the API key.

Flow:
1. Agent: POST /api/v1/magic-link (Bearer API key) → returns {url, expires_at}
2. User clicks URL (e.g. https://wikihub.globalbr.ai/auth/magic/<token>)
3. Server validates token, sets session cookie, redirects to / (or ?next=)
4. Token marked used_at; cannot be reused

Design:
- New MagicLink model: id, user_id, token_hash (SHA-256), expires_at, used_at, created_at
- Token: secrets.token_urlsafe(32) → only hash stored in DB
- TTL: 10 minutes default, overrideable via ttl_seconds param (max 60 min)
- On consume: atomic check+mark used_at; constant-time compare
- Audit log entry on both creation and consumption
- Rate limit creation (e.g. 10/hour per user) — MVP can skip
- Update all agent surfaces (AGENTS.md, /AGENTS.md route, /llms.txt, MCP tools)
- e2e test: create link, GET /auth/magic/<token>, verify session cookie set, second GET returns 410

title: Merge flow is not implemented end to end despite verification need
description:
User explicitly requested verification of the whole merge flow.

Current repo inspection on 2026-04-22 found:
- no obvious merge/proposal/review routes in app/routes
- no merge models in app/models.py
- no e2e coverage for proposal/diff -> merge application
- existing related ticket wikihub-wp9w is a design ticket for third-party collaboration, not an implemented merge flow

This task should clarify and then either:
1. point verification at the real implemented merge surface if one exists elsewhere, or
2. track that merge flow is currently missing and define the smallest concrete MVP flow to build and verify.

This is separate from public-edit, which was verified working on production.

title: Reader page-share modal mishandles email invite responses
description:
Workflow audit finding from 2026-04-22.

The page-level share flow in `reader.html` assumes a `grant`-style response, but the API returns `{invited: email, pattern, role}` for unknown emails. Result: inviting by email from the reader UI is inconsistent/broken even though the backend accepts it.

Likely fix:
- update the page-share modal JS to handle pending-invite responses cleanly
- or normalize the backend response shape for page-share invites
- add an e2e/browser regression for inviting an unknown email from the page share UI

Evidence:
- app/templates/reader.html page-share JS
- app/routes/api_wikis.py invite response path
- workflow audit report /tmp/wikihub-workflow-audit.md
notes: Claimed from workflow audit follow-through on 2026-04-22. Fixing reader page-share invite response handling.

title: Explore: show recently updated wikis
description: Add a way on Explore to browse recently updated wikis, not just featured/top-starred content. Optionally consider recently active people as a later extension, but keep the initial implementation focused on recently updated wikis and a clear recency signal.

title: Review + deploy bde3870 (curator anon gate, CURATOR_ENABLED, admin index)
description:
Commit bde3870 on branch cli-installer closes wikihub-mzoh, wikihub-2jn.2, wikihub-3w46. Not pushed/deployed — needs Jacob's review first.

## Review checklist
1. migrations/2026-04-23_admin_flag_and_settings.sql — idempotent guards look right, jacobcole admin seed correct. Apply on prod BEFORE git pull/restart (command in file header).
2. app/templates/reader.html — curator panel wrap didn't break layout (recurring-issue file, see wikihub-bnj). Diff HTML shape on a reader page.
3. /admin index page (app/templates/admin_index.html) — load in browser as jacobcole, confirm links work, confirm curator toggle flips flag.
4. app/__init__.py — idempotent ALTER users ADD is_admin on startup won't break boot if migration already ran.
5. POST /api/v1/admin/settings — CSRF-exempt via api_bp; confirm non-admin logged-in user can't bypass admin check.

## Local smoke
source .venv/bin/activate
SECRET_KEY=dev DATABASE_URL=postgresql://localhost/wikihub REPOS_DIR=./repos ADMIN_TOKEN=devtoken flask --app wsgi.py run --port 5100

Hit /admin, toggle curator off → reader panel disappears → toggle back on → reappears.

## Deploy sequence
1. Apply migration on prod: ssh -i ~/.ssh/wikihub-dev-key ubuntu@54.145.123.7 'sudo -u postgres psql -d wikihub -f -' < migrations/2026-04-23_admin_flag_and_settings.sql
2. git push origin cli-installer (or merge to main first)
3. ssh deploy: cd /opt/wikihub-app && git pull && sudo systemctl restart wikihub
4. curl -s -o /dev/null -w '%{http_code}' https://wikihub.md/  # expect 200
5. agent-browser smoke-test /admin on prod

## Related
- wikihub-42po (global Curator entrypoint) left open — needs design sketch from Jacob.

title: import pipeline for Obsidian vaults and markdown repos
description: add Obsidian vault and plain markdown repo import. discover and convert [[wikilinks]] (already supported in renderer), preserve folder structure. extend existing zip upload flow. inspired by gbrain's discovery + import pipeline.

title: Lightsail box undersized (1 vCPU / 1GB) — upgrade or move
description:
Box at 54.145.123.7 is a 1 vCPU / 1GB Lightsail instance. Even after the 2026-04-25 nginx bot-throttle fix, sustained load average stays around 2.5+ when both gunicorn workers are busy. Memory: 911MB total, ~690MB used + 268MB swap. We're running close to OOM constantly.

## Options
1. Upgrade in place — Lightsail next tier is 2 vCPU / 4 GB (~10/mo more). One restart.
2. Move to a regular EC2 t4g.small (cheaper at 2vCPU 2GB, ARM)
3. Move to Hetzner CX21 (~5/mo, 2 vCPU / 4 GB, far better $/perf)
4. Add a second box behind a load balancer (overkill until traffic grows)

## Recommended
Hetzner CX21. Same provider class as testcortex (5.78.116.192) which Jacob already uses. One-time migration cost; ongoing bill drops.

## Acceptance
- [ ] Decide
- [ ] Execute migration/upgrade
- [ ] Confirm load avg <1.0 under normal traffic

title: Show lock/sharing icon on sidebar pages based on visibility and grants
description: Sidebar page list should show visibility state per-page: lock icon for private, share/people icon for pages shared with specific users, link icon for unlisted, no icon for public. Currently the sidebar shows all pages identically — you can't tell at a glance which are private, shared, or public. This is important context when browsing someone else's wiki where you've been granted access to specific pages.

title: Security audit: scan wikihub.md for leaked Jacob/IdeaFlow/Anthropic confidential data
description:
Quick paranoia check — wikihub.md has been a heavy-use scratchpad for ~weeks of dogfooding, plus imports from various sources (jeremynixon, harrisonqian, jemoka, listhub migrate, etc.). High likelihood that something sensitive leaked into a public wiki, into commit history, or into a logged feedback submission.

## What to scan

### 1. All public wiki content (highest risk)
For every wiki with visibility='public' or 'public-edit', grep the bare git repo + working tree for:
```
- API keys: 'sk-ant-', 'sk-proj-', 'wh_', 'AIza', 'ghp_', 'github_pat_', 'AKIA', 'pk_live_', 'sk_live_', 'rk_live_'
  Specifically Jacob's known ones (per ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md):
    - Deepgram: d36af20407dcf9f7037d697ad5ad5bda48e178e3
    - NoteStream: ns_259adf003da00bbbea82e3725db8353d019584506d5e4d970639b371c32da056
    - Thoughtstream/IdeaFlow: ts_g-pmvUUi581WIoAGSamkqVWMzYpezbouVJ5EAw1wh8w
- Passwords / SSH key contents: 'BEGIN RSA', 'BEGIN OPENSSH', 'BEGIN EC', 'PRIVATE KEY'
- Internal hostnames / IPs that should not be public:
    - 100.99.121.73 (Mac Mini Tailscale)
    - 100.109.59.66 (M3 Tailscale)
    - 5.78.116.192 / iPsjged75eQnan_aBdVtAXiB (testcortex root password)
    - 3.216.129.34, 54.145.123.7 (server IPs are tolerable but credentials adjacent are not)
- Email: jacobcole4000@gmail.com is fine to leave; renzuseai@gmail.com or other personal addresses worth a sweep
- IdeaFlow / Noos / Thoughtstream internal: 'noos-internal', 'ideaflow-confidential', 'investor', 'cap table', 'pre-money', 'term sheet', 'salary'
- Customer / user PII: any apparent real names + emails + dates that look like contacts list
```

Run as: scripts/audit_wikis.sh that iterates Wiki.query.filter(visibility.in_(['public','public-edit','unlisted','unlisted-edit'])) and greps each repo.

### 2. Commit messages / authorship
`git log --all` across every wiki repo for the same patterns. An attacker with git read can see secrets that were committed and 'deleted' — the file removal doesn't expunge them.
For any hits: `git filter-repo` (or BFG) to expunge from history; rotate the credential immediately.

### 3. Feedback table
SELECT * FROM feedback WHERE body ILIKE '%password%' OR body ILIKE '%api%key%' OR body ILIKE '%secret%' OR contact_email IS NOT NULL — review manually. Anything posted by anonymous users that looks like a leak.

### 4. Beads issues exported to /opt/wikihub-app/.beads/issues.jsonl
This file is committed. Recent commits I made today reference IPs and tokens (e.g. wikihub-17v7 mentions specific Anthropic budget caps; wikihub-9ntv lists nginx config). Audit that nothing in beads exports an actual key.

### 5. Server logs + app logs reachable from the web
- Verify /admin/* is gated (it should be) and not crawlable.
- Verify /api/v1/admin/claude-auth/* requires ADMIN_TOKEN.
- Verify /var/log/nginx/access.log is NOT served via nginx accidentally.
- Check if any 5xx error pages echo environment variables, file paths, or the SECRET_KEY.

### 6. Cross-product contamination
Jacob runs noos, listhub, listwise, thoughtstream-notes, voice-assistant, openclaw, etc. — all on related infra. Look for content in wikihub that references internal noos endpoints, OAuth client secrets, Anthropic creds shared between projects, or jcortex/openclaw operational details.

### 7. Imported wikis (3rd-party content we re-host)
- harrisonqian/awesome (imported) — was the bot-scrape target. Check whether the import dragged in anything sensitive (.env, .git/config with private remote URLs).
- jeremynixon migrate
- jemoka/knowledge-base
- Any wiki with visibility='public-edit' — anyone (incl. attackers) could have written secrets into it.

### 8. The 'wikihub' user's own wiki
/@wikihub/wikihub — ships with index.md, log.md, schema.md as defaults. If Jacob (or an agent) ever edited this with internal docs, those are public. Skim.

## Output
A single audit report at memory/research/wikihub-leak-audit-YYYY-MM-DD.md with:
- Per-finding: severity (low/medium/high/critical), what, where (wiki + path + commit if applicable), recommended action
- Top-line summary
- Any credential found gets rotated BEFORE the report is shared with anyone

## Tools that already exist
- bd close <ticket> for tracking remediations
- git log --all -p in each repo (be careful — this scans deleted content too)
- scripts/scrape_gdocs.py is in the repo as an example of how repos are stored

## Acceptance
- [ ] Audit script committed under scripts/audit_secrets.py
- [ ] Audit report written
- [ ] Any high/critical findings: rotate credential, expunge from history, document in audit report
- [ ] Add a pre-commit hook (server-side, on the post-receive) to reject pushes containing common secret patterns going forward

## Severity rationale
P1 not P0 because: a thorough manual review likely won't find something catastrophic given how Jacob works (he's careful enough to put real secrets in 1Password, not in markdown). But the box has been a heavy-use scratchpad, the imported wikis are wild-card, and at least one ('public-edit') visibility class lets anyone write content. Every day this isn't done is a day that leaks could be indexed by the same GPTBot/ClaudeBot crawlers we just throttled. So: this week, not this hour.

title: Build Karpathy import tool: auto-generate log.md + schema.md from imported wikis
description:
When importing external content (blogs, Google Sites, GitHub Pages) into WikiHub, automatically generate:

1. **log.md** — retrospective timeline reconstructed from dates in filenames/content. Each dated entry gets a log line: '## 2017-02-01 — Added: Ways to Discover Contrarian Truths'. This creates the Karpathy activity feed pattern even for imported content.

2. **schema.md** — auto-generated from analyzing the content structure. Describes what the wiki covers, how it's organized, and instructions for an agent maintaining it.

3. **Ripple updates** — when a new raw source is ingested, the agent should update relevant wiki/ compiled pages AND append to log.md.

The key Karpathy mechanic: 'Ingesting one source can update 10-15 wiki pages.' The schema tells the agent HOW to do this. log.md records THAT it happened.

This should be a reusable tool/skill, not one-off code. Every wiki import should produce this structure automatically.

Reference: Karpathy gist https://gist.github.com/karpathy/442a6bf555914893e9891c11519de94f
See also: memory/reference_llmwiki.md for the three-layer pattern.

title: Add /tickets page — public beads ticket viewer
description: Add a web page (linked from footer) that renders the beads issue tracker as a public ticket board. Shows open issues, priorities, status. Could be a simple server-rendered page that reads .beads/issues.jsonl. Transparency for users about what's being worked on.
close_reason: Live at /roadmap — fetches issues.jsonl from GitHub, filterable tree with detail panel

title: Graph view: unbounded zoomable canvas instead of fixed bounding box
description: The graph view is too cramped with many notes. Replace the fixed bounding box with an unbounded canvas inside a clearly defined border-radius rectangle. When the mouse is inside the graph area and scrolling, zoom in/out smoothly into an unbounded inner graph space. Nodes remain clickable. This should make large wikis much more navigable — the current approach tries to fit everything into a tiny box which gets chaotic with many notes. Alternative considered: just making the sidebar wider, but unbounded zoom is the better UX.

title: [Agent UX] API discoverability: /api/v1/openapi.json + X-Request-ID + consistent error envelopes
description:
# Observed

Independent agent review 2026-04-23 noted three inter-related discoverability gaps:

## 1. No OpenAPI schema

\`GET /api/v1/openapi.json\` → 404. Discovery requires reading three separate places with minor drift:
- \`/agents\` HTML page
- \`/llms.txt\` root file
- \`app/routes/api*.py\` source

**Fix:** use \`flask-smorest\` or \`apispec\` to auto-generate OpenAPI 3.1 from the Flask route defs. Publish at \`/api/v1/openapi.json\`. Link from \`/agents\` and \`/llms.txt\`.

Bonus: agent clients (OpenAI function-calling, Anthropic tools) can consume OpenAPI and autogenerate tool schemas.

## 2. No X-Request-ID on responses

When something fails — especially the \`/history\` 500 — agents have no request-ID to quote when filing a bug. Server logs and client error get disconnected.

**Fix:** add Flask middleware that generates a uuid per request, echoes on response header \`X-Request-ID\`, and tags every log line.

## 3. Inconsistent error envelopes

Observed:
- 404 from API routes → JSON \`{error, message}\` ✅
- 405 Method Not Allowed → Flask default HTML ❌
- 500 from broken /history → JSON but zero diagnostic ❌
- Cloudflare 1010 → HTML block page ❌ (separate issue wikihub-8dz)

**Fix:** register a global Flask errorhandler for 4xx/5xx on \`/api/*\` blueprints that always returns \`{error, message, request_id}\` JSON. Ensure Cloudflare bypasses /api/* for bot-fight (from wikihub-8dz).

# Done when

- [ ] /api/v1/openapi.json live and linked
- [ ] X-Request-ID echoed on every response
- [ ] All /api/* errors return JSON envelope

title: DM / messaging between users — Twitter-style integrated chat
description: Agent-friendly messaging: 'send Harrison a link to my orchestrator skill'. Should feel integrated like Twitter DMs — not a separate app. Consider overlap with existing OpenChat project (~/code/open-chat or similar). Options: (1) build into wikihub directly, (2) integrate OpenChat as a module, (3) separate service with deep wikihub links. Key use case: sharing wiki pages/content between users with context. Future: agent-to-agent messaging for orchestration across users.

title: Signup: username auto-derive from email local-part (JS live prefill)
description:
When user types into the email field on /auth/signup, auto-populate the username field with the sanitized local-part (e.g. tejas@ideaflow.io → tejas). Stop auto-updating once user manually edits username (JS dirty flag). Reuse the sanitization logic from google_callback (strip non-alphanumeric, enforce 2-40 chars). This is the follow-up to the field reorder — username prefill makes the form feel like one-field signup.

Related: wikihub-3i9i (filed earlier with same scope). Consider consolidating.

title: Add share button to page view (reader.html) for wiki owners
Create Karpathy-style LLM wiki from Jemoka's notes
close_reason: Wiki live at wikihub.globalbr.ai/@jemoka/knowledge-base — 3166 pages, Karpathy 3-layer format, daily log + structured notes

title: No server-side username validation — accepts XSS, spaces, path traversal
description: The signup form's pattern attribute ([a-z0-9_-]+) is client-side only. The server accepts usernames with <script> tags, spaces, uppercase, and path traversal (../../../etc/passwd). These users appear on the People page. XSS is mitigated by Jinja2 auto-escaping, but the usernames shouldn't be allowed. Fix: add server-side regex validation matching the client pattern.
close_reason: added server-side regex ^[a-z0-9_-]+$ with 40 char max on both signup form and API /accounts endpoint

title: TOC double-escapes ampersands in right sidebar
description: When a heading contains an ampersand, the table of contents in the right sidebar shows it as literal ampersand-amp-semicolon instead of the & symbol. The heading text itself renders correctly. Verified on tools-stack (Homerow & Vimium, Notes & Knowledge) and awesome-claude-skills/wiki/productivity (file & knowledge management, task & workflow, resume & career). The HTML source confirms the TOC links contain double-escaped entities.

title: Sidebar folders open and close unpredictably while navigating
description: Audit and fix the left-hand sidebar behavior where clicking around causes seemingly random folders to open or close. Scope: folder open-state persistence, active-path auto-expansion, sort/re-render interactions, and click/event delegation in the reader sidebar. Related to existing sidebar ordering bugs but should be tracked separately because the user-facing symptom is unstable open/closed state rather than just wrong sort order.

title: Track small-team Google Drive replacement readiness assessment and gap-closure plan
description:
Recent user request asked for a realistic assessment of what it would take for WikiHub to replace Google Drive for a small team.

This ticket is the durable planning artifact that ties together the readiness report and the concrete follow-up work.

Scope:
- capture the current readiness assessment
- identify the minimum bar for inviting a 4-5 person team
- rank blockers vs nice-to-haves
- map gaps to existing/open tickets where possible
- spin new gap-closure tickets where no bead exists yet

Expected outputs:
- readiness score / report
- top blockers and strengths
- quick wins
- recommendation on whether the team rollout should happen now or after specific fixes ship

title: nginx limit_req should key on Cloudflare client IP, not edge IP
description:
Just added per-IP rate limits on /api/v1/agent/chat etc. ($binary_remote_addr). Behind Cloudflare, that variable is the Cloudflare edge IP, which rotates per request. So a single attacker can effectively bypass the per-IP cap by hitting different edges.

## Fix
Use ngx_http_realip_module:
```
set_real_ip_from 173.245.48.0/20;   # Cloudflare ranges
... (full Cloudflare list)
real_ip_header CF-Connecting-IP;
real_ip_recursive on;
```

Cloudflare publishes its IP ranges at https://www.cloudflare.com/ips-v4 and ips-v6. Either:
1. Static include in nginx conf (refresh quarterly)
2. cron job that fetches + reloads nginx

## Files
- /etc/nginx/conf.d/cloudflare-realip.conf (NEW)
- Document in repo per wikihub-9ntv

## Acceptance
- [ ] limit_req fires correctly when same client retries from different Cloudflare edges
- [ ] Application logs (and Flask request.remote_addr) show real client IP, not 104.x/172.x

title: [Agent UX] Read-side API completeness: list-own-wikis, README↔index alias, HEAD, bulk-read
description:
# Observed

Independent agent review 2026-04-23 found several gaps in the READ-side API:

## 1. No way to list my own wikis

Given only an API key, an agent cannot discover which wikis it can write to:
- \`GET /api/v1/wikis\` → 405 Method Not Allowed
- \`GET /api/v1/users/<name>/wikis\` → 404
- Must scrape \`/@user\` HTML to enumerate

**Fix:** \`GET /api/v1/wikis?owner=me\` (or \`?owner=<username>\`) returning the same shape as the single-wiki GET.

## 2. No README.md → index.md alias

Agents from a GitHub mental model call \`GET /api/v1/wikis/.../pages/README.md\` expecting the landing page. Returns 404. The canonical path is \`index.md\`.

**Fix:** treat \`README.md\` and \`README\` as aliases for \`index.md\`. OR document prominently.

## 3. No HEAD /pages/<path>

Agents can't check staleness without pulling full content. A \`HEAD\` returning \`ETag\` + \`Last-Modified\` would let them skip big reads when their cache is fresh.

**Fix:** wire HEAD to the same handler as GET but return headers only.

## 4. No bulk-read endpoint (counterpart to the bulk-write ticket wikihub-2zh)

Reading a whole wiki means N round-trips. For agents doing RAG or wiki-mirroring, this is O(N) latency.

**Fix:** \`GET /api/v1/wikis/<o>/<s>/bulk-read?paths=a.md,b.md\` or NDJSON stream. Workaround exists today via \`/@o/s/llms-full.txt\` but that's HTML-oriented, not REST.

# Done when

- [ ] list-my-wikis endpoint
- [ ] README↔index alias (or clear doc)
- [ ] HEAD /pages/<path>
- [ ] bulk-read JSON endpoint

title: No way to name/rename a page when creating from web UI
description: When creating a new page from the web UI, there's no clear way to set the page title/name. The page path becomes the title but there's no rename functionality. Need: title field on create form, rename option on existing pages (both web UI and keeping the existing API PATCH endpoint working).

title: [Agent UX] default_visibility param on POST /wikis + POST /wikis scaffolding improvements
description:
# Observed

POST /api/v1/wikis with \"template\":\"structured\" creates index.md, log.md, schema.md as PRIVATE by default. I wanted them public for the picortex wiki, so after creation I had to call /bulk-visibility to flip them. Two write calls instead of one, and I needed to know the scaffolded paths ahead of time to flip them.

# Proposal

1. Accept \`default_visibility\` in POST /wikis:
\`\`\`json
{
  \"slug\": \"picortex\",
  \"title\": \"...\",
  \"template\": \"structured\",
  \"default_visibility\": \"public\"
}
\`\`\`
Apply to all scaffolded pages. Default remains \"private\" for backwards compat.

2. Return the scaffolded page list in the POST /wikis response:
\`\`\`json
{
  \"id\": 149, \"slug\": \"picortex\", ...,
  \"scaffolded_pages\": [
    {\"path\": \"index.md\", \"visibility\": \"public\"},
    {\"path\": \"log.md\", \"visibility\": \"public\"},
    {\"path\": \"schema.md\", \"visibility\": \"public\"}
  ]
}
\`\`\`

Agents don't have to guess what got created.

# Done when

- [ ] default_visibility accepted and applied
- [ ] scaffolded_pages returned in POST /wikis response
- [ ] /agents page shows an example

title: KaTeX does not render LaTeX align/paren delimiters
description: Pages using standard LaTeX delimiters like \begin{align}...\end{align} and \(...\) show raw LaTeX source instead of rendered math. The KaTeX auto-render config only includes dollar-sign delimiters. Verified on @jemoka/knowledge-base/raw/concept/kbhbayes_theorem which has Bayes theorem formulas displayed as plain text. Additionally the markdown renderer appears to strip backslashes before parentheses, converting \( to ( before KaTeX can process them.

title: recently updated feed on wiki homepage and profiles
description: show last N modified pages on wiki homepage and user profiles. data already available from git history. render as a 'recently updated' section like Farzapedia's homepage sidebar.

title: Polish people cards with visibility badges and richer profile metadata
description: Follow up on the first-pass people browsing feature by adding spec-consistent visibility badges on explore/profile cards, richer personal-wiki metadata on person cards, and finalizing mobile polish for the person-page sidebar.

title: Restructure Jeremy Nixon wiki to Karpathy LLM Wiki format with daily logs
Add one-click magic-link sign-in for agents
description: Let an authenticated agent generate a short-lived one-click sign-in link for the account owner, so the browser session can be established without typing credentials or pasting API keys. Update the spec and add e2e coverage.
acceptance_criteria: API endpoint returns a one-click login URL for the authenticated account; visiting the link logs the user into a browser session and invalidates the token; link expires; spec updated; e2e coverage added.
notes: Implement short-lived single-use magic login links for agent-generated browser sign-in, update spec, and add e2e coverage.
close_reason: Implemented short-lived single-use magic sign-in links via API and browser route, documented the agent flow, updated the spec, and added e2e coverage.

title: article type system with per-type rendering (person, book, place)
description: add a type: frontmatter key (person, book, place, artifact, assessment) with per-type rendering. person gets infobox, book gets cover, place gets map link. builds on infobox feature. inspired by Farzapedia's category system.

title: Epic: GitHub URL → instant LLM wiki (paste-to-view, with optional pushback)
description:
Status: NEEDS JACOB REVIEW before implementation. Full plan captured below.

## Core concept

Treat a WikiHub URL like `/gh/<owner>/<repo>` as a **cacheable, editable "view" on a GitHub repo**. Read-through on first hit, editable after. Pushback to GitHub is a later phase, not v1.

Framing: "paste GitHub URL → instant LLM wiki." A key acquisition path — most users already have knowledge in public repos and should see WikiHub value without manual page creation.

## Related existing tickets

- wikihub-0cxg (P1) — Onboarding: paste public GitHub URL → generated wiki  → matches Phase 1
- wikihub-40tr (P1) — GitHub↔WikiHub sync + pushback (PR vs direct push) → matches Phases 4 + 5
- wikihub-chil (P1) — Private repo import with selective exposure → matches Phase 6
- wikihub-ibt.2 (P2) — URL/repo connect plumbing. NOTE: ticket says "plumbing exists in git_sync.py" but that is WRONG — git_sync.py manages internal bare repos only, not external clone. External `git clone` still needs to be written.
- wikihub-ibt.1 (P2) — Paste unstructured text → LLM-generated wiki → reusable for Phase 2

## Phasing

| Phase | Scope | Maps to | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1. MVP paste-URL | Form on landing page + `POST /import/github`. Shallow-clone → filter text/md files → copy into new wiki bare repo via existing `apply_repo_changes`. Lands at `/@<you>/<repo>`. | wikihub-0cxg | 1–2 days |
| 2. LLM-generated structure | After clone, Claude pass generates index.md + overview / key-files / getting-started pages. Platform key, budget-capped. | wikihub-ibt.1 (adapted) | 3–5 days |
| 3. URL endpoint | `/gh/<owner>/<repo>` — first hit clones and redirects, later hits serve cached. Imports land under `@<gh-owner>` as `claimable=True`, source-stamped. Real GitHub owner can later claim via OAuth. | NEW TICKET NEEDED | 2 days |
| 4. Refresh from GitHub | "Synced 6h ago · refresh" button. Fetch → diff → apply non-conflicting upstream changes. | wikihub-40tr (one-way half) | 3 days |
| 5. Pushback | "PR this upstream" on each page. Requires GitHub OAuth. | wikihub-40tr (bidi half) | 1 week |
| 6. Private import | GitHub App for private repos + selective public exposure via `.wikihub/export.md`. | wikihub-chil | separate design |

## URL-endpoint design (Phase 3) — options

Option A: **`/gh/<owner>/<repo>`** — short, lowercase, distinct from `@` user namespace. Jacob/Claude preference.
Option B: **`/github.com/<owner>/<repo>`** — mirrors DeepWiki; self-explanatory. Downside: `github.com` as a path segment is weird.
Option C: **No separate route** — just `/@<owner>/<repo>` with `source=github` flag, relying on the synthetic-account namespace.

First-visit behavior:
1. Check if `@<owner>/<repo>` wiki exists → redirect.
2. If not: enqueue clone, show "importing…" page with live status, redirect when ready. (Synchronous clone won't work for big repos — Phase 1.5 needs a background worker.)
3. Imported wiki owned by synthetic `@<owner>` account marked `claimable=True, source=github:<owner>/<repo>`. Real GitHub owner claims later via OAuth (prove `owner` == GitHub login).

## Open questions (blocking decisions)

1. URL pattern: A (`/gh/...`), B (`/github.com/...`), or C (no separate route)?
2. Is Phase 1 MVP OK as "dumb mirror of markdown files" with no LLM restructure — or is the LLM pass (Phase 2) table-stakes for the first paste-URL demo?
3. Pushback (Phase 5) — PR model or direct-push? PR is safer but needs OAuth from day one.

## Risks / design details flagged

- Clone timeouts for big repos — need background worker by Phase 1.5.
- Rate-limiting abuse — anyone can trigger a clone of anything. Need IP/global rate limit.
- License/attribution — auto-extract LICENSE into sidebar, stamp source URL on every page.
- Namespace collisions — `@mitchellh` on WikiHub ≠ GitHub's mitchellh. Synthetic-account namespace `github:<owner>` vs user-facing `@<owner>`.
- Size caps for Phase 1: < 100MB, < 5k files.
- File filter: skip `.git`, `node_modules`, binaries > 1MB, lockfiles.

## Next steps (after Jacob reviews)

1. Jacob answers the 3 open questions.
2. Create 2 new sub-tickets (URL endpoint, LLM restructure) and link wikihub-0cxg / 40tr / chil / ibt.1 / ibt.2 as children of this epic.
3. Start Phase 1 MVP.

title: Editor preview tab shows plaintext instead of rendered markdown
description: The Preview tab in the editor (editor.html) shows raw markdown text via textContent, not rendered output. Needs either a client-side markdown renderer or an AJAX call to the server's render endpoint. Related to wikihub-18e (editor QA). This is the biggest UX gap in the editing experience.

title: Account dropdown menu disappears before clicking menu items
description: The account dropdown menu in the nav only works if you click the avatar icon. Hovering opens it but moving the mouse down to the menu items causes it to disappear before you can click them. Likely a gap between the trigger and the dropdown that breaks the :hover chain, or the mouseenter/mouseleave handlers are too aggressive. Needs a hover delay or bridge element.

title: Meta: document all agent instruction surfaces that need sync
Public edit (public-edit visibility) not working from web UI
description: Pages with public-edit visibility should be editable by any logged-in user, not just the owner. The web UI edit button may not appear or the route may reject non-owners even when the page allows public editing. Confirmed: API correctly rejects non-owner edits on non-public-edit pages. Need to verify the public-edit flow works end-to-end.

title: Decision: Cloudflare Email Service vs Resend (resolved)
description:
Decision log, 2026-04-21:

**Decision:** Use Cloudflare Email Service for wikihub (not Resend).

**Reasoning:**
- $5/mo Workers Paid already committed (unlocks containers, D1 unlimited, Hyperdrive, etc.)
- Email is $0/mo for first 3k/month, then $0.35/1k flat — cheaper than Resend at every scale
- Domain onboarding auto-creates SPF/DKIM/DMARC in the Cloudflare zone (wikihub.md is already there)
- Single vendor / single dashboard / single billing

**Trade-offs accepted:**
- Public beta — less battle-tested than Resend for deliverability
- From Flask (Lightsail), we use REST API instead of Workers binding — slightly less elegant than native binding but fully supported

**Integration path:**
- REST endpoint: `POST https://api.cloudflare.com/client/v4/accounts/{account_id}/email-service/send`
- Auth: Bearer token with Email Sending scope
- Send domain: wikihub.md (to be onboarded in CF dashboard)
- Sender addresses: noreply@wikihub.md, invites@wikihub.md

**Considered alternatives:**
- Resend: Cortex pattern, known good, but $20/mo floor for custom domain (free tier 1-domain limit blocked wikihub.md). Would add a second vendor.
- MailChannels: free tier for CF Workers ended Aug 2024 — not viable.
- AWS SES: wikihub is on Lightsail but switching would require separate identity verification + warm-up. No infrastructure lock-in win.

See wikihub-exj1 for implementation.

title: Editor route crashes on production: llm_api_key_encrypted column missing
description:
Navigating to any /edit page on production (e.g. https://wikihub.globalbr.ai/@harrisonqian/tools-stack/index/edit) throws a 500 error:

sqlalchemy.exc.ProgrammingError: (psycopg2.errors.UndefinedColumn) column users.llm_api_key_encrypted does not exist

The code references a column that hasn't been added to the production database schema. A migration is needed to add this column.

Screenshot: /tmp/stress_edit_17_editor.png

title: IdeaFlow import: linkify Product design meetings child pages
description:
The imported `product/product-design-meetings.md` page in the IdeaFlow wiki still has plain-text child meeting entries like `Product design meeting (2023-04-27)` instead of actual links.

Current state after the Notion cleanup pass: raw `[child_page]` markers are gone, but unmatched child pages were degraded to plain titles rather than linked correctly.

Fix: fetch the child pages from Notion, create/import the missing meeting pages into the IdeaFlow wiki, and update the parent page to use internal wikilinks for those meetings.

title: Add browser-driven core web workflow smoke suite
description: Current e2e coverage is strong at the Flask/client level, but we still lack a committed browser-driven happy-path smoke that exercises the real web UI for owner login, wiki creation, folder/page creation, editing, sharing, and collaborator read/write access. Add a lightweight maintained smoke path so UI regressions are caught before deploy.

title: Use underscores for wiki page URLs
description: Canonical page URLs should use underscores instead of spaces; accept underscores in routing/API; redirect space/%20 URLs to underscore form; update URL generation and wikilink rendering.
close_reason: Switched page URL generation to underscores, added URL decode for underscores, canonical redirect for space URLs, updated wikilinks/templates/API/agent surfaces, and verified via tests + browser.

title: EPIC: Programmatic test coverage parity — agent can verify before human tests
description:
Goal: **an agent (me, codex, any future session) can verify every user-facing flow programmatically before Jacob has to click through anything.** Today we have 45 e2e tests, but real bugs keep slipping past because whole flows are untested.

## Why now

Recent bugs that a test would have caught:
- **wikihub-35gh** — prod git push fails because pre-receive hook can't import Flask. No git push e2e test exists.
- **/people 404 on user subdomains** — subdomain test only covered apex paths.
- **Reader share modal** — audit misflagged the invited-response handling because no JS-level test exists to prove the modal works.

These are all programmatic flows. If we had coverage, I could catch them before shipping and before Jacob has to manually test.

## Coverage gaps (what to add, in priority order)

### Critical (P0-class bugs are slipping through here)
1. **Git clone + push e2e** — spin a test client, clone a wiki over Smart HTTP, make a commit, push, verify the hook ran, verify DB-sync landed. Must run against a real pre-receive hook installation.
2. **Subdomain matrix** — for every global route (/people, /shared, /explore, /settings, /auth/*, /.well-known/*), test both apex AND user-subdomain host. Parameterize over routes; a new main_bp route should automatically get tested on subdomains.
3. **Full browser-level smoke** via Playwright or chrome-devtools headless — every click path a real user takes (signup → confirmation email → click link → land on wiki). Tests need to cover the JS, not just the HTML.

### High
4. **Google OAuth round-trip** — mock the Google OIDC endpoints, simulate the callback, verify user creation, email verification state, and pending-invite materialization.
5. **MCP server tool invocations** — stand up the MCP server locally, invoke each of the 19 tools, assert responses. Source: mcp-server/ directory.
6. **Editor save flow from the UI** — POST to the editor endpoint with form data, verify Page row updates, verify git commit landed.
7. **File upload (binary)** — multipart POST of PNG/PDF/etc, verify served correctly with right content-type, verify ACL.

### Medium
8. **Wiki deletion cascade** — delete a wiki with stars/forks/wikilinks, verify referential integrity.
9. **Fork operations** — fork a wiki, push to fork, verify source is untouched.
10. **Reader page rendering** — given markdown with wikilinks/KaTeX/footnotes, verify the rendered HTML has the expected structure.
11. **Real email delivery** — live Cloudflare Email Service integration test (gated by env var so CI doesn't spam).

## The maintenance rule (to prevent regressing to this state)

**Add to AGENTS.md: "every bug fix MUST include a regression test that would have failed before the fix."** Specifically:

- If a bug is filed (`bd type=bug`), the fix PR must add or modify a test that demonstrates the bug and now passes.
- If the bug is a "programmatically untestable" class (browser behavior, real email, real DNS), the fix must either add the testing infrastructure OR file a dependency ticket for that infra before closing the bug.
- Agents reviewing their own work should explicitly ask: "would my change pass if the exact bug were reintroduced?" If not, the test is insufficient.

Ticket hygiene:
- `bd label add <closed-bug-id> needs-regression-test` on any closed bug that didn't get a test
- Monthly sweep: go through `bd list --label needs-regression-test` and add tests or downgrade to "won't fix"

## Tooling asks

- Playwright or chrome-devtools-mcp integrated into the test runner (currently only used ad-hoc)
- A CI that runs the full suite on every push (currently only local + manual)
- A pre-deploy hook that runs at least the auth + git flows before `systemctl restart wikihub`

## Definition of done for this epic

An agent can run `python3 tests/test_e2e.py` and be confident that if it passes, **no user-facing flow is broken**. Today: false. Goal: true. Close this epic only when a fresh agent could take any open ticket and not need human test assistance.

title: Sidebar: drag-and-drop reordering of pages and folders
Curate: General startup resources — free credits, tools, programs for funded startups
description:
Build a meta-list of free/discounted resources available to startups, especially funded ones. Seed categories:

- Cloud credits: GCP for Startups (requires funded/accelerator), AWS Activate, Azure for Startups, Oracle for Startups
- AI/LLM credits: OpenAI, Anthropic, Cohere, Mistral startup programs; NVIDIA's ~80 free hosted AI model APIs (see Twitter-extraction spike for the list — MiniMax M2.7, GLM 5.1, Kimi 2.5, DeepSeek 3.2, GPT-OSS-120B, Sarvam-M)
- SaaS freebies: Notion, Linear, Figma, Vercel, Stripe Atlas, Carta, Brex/Mercury signup bonuses
- Legal/ops: Stripe Atlas, Clerky, Firstbase, Gunderson/Orrick/Cooley flat-fee incorp packages
- YC / accelerator deal pages (YC bookface deals — not public, but many are)
- Free tier + indie programs (GitHub Student/Startup, JetBrains, 1Password Teams)

Each entry: name, what you get, eligibility (funded? pre-seed? post-Series-A?), link.

Format per architecture-decision ticket.
notes: Blocked by architecture-decision ticket. Prefer this list to be the SHOWCASE one — it's the most broadly useful. Check awesome-list audit before starting to avoid duplicating awesome-free-for-dev.

title: Suggestion flow for arbitrary pages, starting with public-edit pages
description: Implement or at least explicitly design a user-facing way to suggest edits to an arbitrary page, with the first concrete target being pages that already allow public edit. Scope: propose a change without directly overwriting live content, show diffs/review affordances, and define how this interacts with the current missing merge flow. This is a narrower execution ticket under the broader collaboration design work.

title: Regression: wiki title vs page title path display broken
Meta contributions log: recent contributions by person, rendered as cards
description:
Feature: a meta-surface on WikiHub (personal and site-wide) that shows recent contributions by person, rendered as cards.

Scope ideas:
- Per-user: on @user profile, a 'recent contributions' feed — pages created, edited, claimed, forked, starred. Each as a card with wiki/page title, snippet, diff summary, timestamp.
- Site-wide: a /recent or /activity feed showing the most active contributors and what they're working on across all public wikis.
- Per-wiki: a 'recent contributors' card on each wiki homepage (who's been editing this).

Card shape (draft):
  [avatar] username · action (created/edited/claimed) · wiki/page link
  [snippet of the change or page intro]
  [timestamp] · [+N / -M lines] · [diff link]

Data source: we already have an audit_log table (app/models.py) — confirm it captures enough (actor, action, target, timestamp) or extend. Pages have created/updated timestamps too.

Relation to curation epic (wikihub-zdy1): a contributions feed IS a form of curation — it surfaces what's being worked on, and curated lists are themselves pages that get edited. This view makes curation activity legible and gives social proof for contributors on public-edit wikis.
notes:
Relates to curation epic. Also adjacent to wikihub-dz5 (community index wiki).

IMPORTANT overlap identified 2026-04-24: this ticket overlaps heavily with wikihub-6b1 (Per-user activity feeds). ry5w is best understood as the CARD RENDERING LAYER on top of 6b1's timeline data. Don't build a separate data path — ry5w consumes the /@user/activity API from 6b1. If 6b1's scope expands to include card-style rendering, consider closing ry5w as duplicate.

Also connects to wikihub-9bi (follow users + personalized feed) — the card rendering should work identically on a user profile (who-they-are) and on a follower's feed (what-I-should-see). Same component, different data source.

Open design questions:
- Privacy: does the feed include edits to private pages? (default no, with opt-in)
- Anonymous contributions (wikihub-7b2r): how are anonymous-but-claimable edits rendered?
- Pagination/infinite scroll vs daily-digest format
- RSS/Atom feed for subscribers

title: private wikis leak page existence and index content to unauthenticated users
description:
Two related access control issues with private wikis:

1. **Index page visible on private wikis** — if a wiki is fully private, visiting the wiki index still renders the page. It should deny access entirely (redirect to login or show a 'no access' message).

2. **Direct links to private pages show empty page shell** — if you have a direct link to a private page (e.g. from a search engine or shared URL), the route renders the page template with no content instead of showing a clear 'no access' or 'this page is private' message. This leaks that the page exists and looks like a bug to the user.

Expected behavior:
- Private wiki index: 403/404 or redirect, not a rendered page
- Direct link to private page: clear 'no access' message, not an empty page shell

title: Orchestrator skill for tmux session management
History/commit endpoints run unbounded git log per request — cache or paginate
description:
ROOT CAUSE of the 2026-04-25 incident where wikihub.md degraded to 15-20s page loads. AI crawlers (GPTBot 1147 req, ClaudeBot 77, SemrushBot 104, MJ12bot 40, GoogleOther 38) scraped /history and /commit/<sha> endpoints across all wikis. Each request runs git log --max-count=50 in-process, pegging the 1-vCPU box and holding Postgres txns open mid-request (showed as 'idle in transaction' with ClientRead wait_event).

## Files
- app/routes/wiki.py — history/folder-history/commit routes
- Probably app/git_sync.py or similar — the git log call site

## Fix options (any one, ideally all)
1. Add functools.lru_cache or DB-backed cache on (wiki_id, path, max_count) → JSON. TTL 1 hour. Most history doesn't change often.
2. Lower default --max-count from 50 to 10 (current shows 50 commits per page; 10 is enough for first paint; pagination handles the rest)
3. Add Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600 to /history and /commit responses so Cloudflare caches them
4. For /commit/<sha>, the response is immutable — Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable

## Acceptance
- [ ] /@harrisonqian/awesome/wiki/.../history responds in <500ms when warm
- [ ] /commit/<sha> responses are Cloudflare-cacheable (immutable)
- [ ] No more 'idle in transaction' cascades on history-heavy traffic

title: [Agent UX][BUG] Search scoping silently broken + list-pages ignores limit/offset
description:
# Two distinct bugs found by an independent agent review 2026-04-23

## 1. Search ?wiki= param silently ignored without ?scope=wiki

Calling \`GET /api/v1/search?q=picortex&wiki=jacobcole/picortex\` returns global results (including \`harrisonqian/awesome\`), NOT just picortex pages. The combo \`?scope=wiki&wiki=owner/slug\` works, but:
- Neither \`/agents\` nor \`/llms.txt\` documents that scope=wiki is required
- Sending \`wiki=...\` without \`scope=...\` fails silently instead of erroring
- Agents that wrote 'scope search to one wiki' code from the README will ship buggy scoping

Source: `app/routes/api_wikis.py:1068` shows the scope= gate.

### Fix
- **Error loudly** when \`wiki=\` is set but \`scope=wiki\` is not (HTTP 400 with \"hint: add scope=wiki\")
- OR **treat \`wiki=\` as implicit scope** (more DWIM)
- Update \`/agents\` and \`/llms.txt\` either way

## 2. List-pages endpoint ignores ?limit and ?offset

\`GET /api/v1/wikis/<o>/<s>/pages?limit=5\` returns all pages (no pagination applied). Route at `app/routes/api_wikis.py:446` has no pagination logic. Fine at 35 pages; catastrophic at 3,500.

### Fix
- Implement \`limit\` (default 100, max 1000) + \`offset\` or cursor-based pagination
- Return \`next\` / \`has_more\` fields in the response envelope
- Integration test

# Done when

- [ ] Search scope validation + docs
- [ ] Pagination on list-pages + response envelope updated

title: Pending invites: share by email before signup
description:
Today the bulk-share endpoint rejects emails with no matching account (wikihub-iga9). Enhancement:

1. Add `PendingInvite(id, wiki_id, pattern, email, role, invited_by, created_at)` table.
2. In POST /share and /share/bulk: if email has no user, store a PendingInvite instead of failing. Return the item in a new 'invited' bucket (distinct from 'failed').
3. On signup (or when a user attaches an email to an existing account): look up PendingInvites by email → materialize as ACL grants, delete the invite rows, audit-log each grant.
4. Surface pending invites in the wiki settings Collaborators list (e.g., 'tejas@ideaflow.io (pending — not yet signed up)').
5. Allow revoking a pending invite.
6. Tests: e2e test covering share-to-unknown-email → signup → access granted.

Blocks Jacob's real-world flow of inviting teammates (tejas, piyush, hari, ridhi @ideaflow.io) before they have accounts.

title: Hover previews: show stub cards + close secondary on reentry to primary
description:
## Problem 1: Stubs flash 'Loading…' then vanish

Wikilinks to non-existent pages have class \`wikilink-broken\` (set by app/renderer.py:321). When hovered, the current code (app/templates/base.html:599) calls the API which returns 404 and the card silently disappears. User expects a dedicated stub card saying the page does not exist yet (and ideally linking to create it).

## Problem 2: Secondary card won't close when returning to primary

Current mouseleave handler in base.html:541-551 keeps a card alive if relatedTarget is any card in the stack. If user hovers card B (depth 1) then moves back to card A (depth 0), card B stays open because A is also in the stack. User expects: leaving card B (even to return to A) closes B.

## Fix plan

1. In the mouseover handler, detect \`.wikilink-broken\` links and open a stub-style card (no fetch, includes 'Stub — page not created' message).
2. Rework the card mouseleave handler to compare depths: if moving to a card at depth d' < my depth, run closeFromDepth(d' + 1). Only keep alive when moving to a deeper (or equal-depth) card.

## Scope

- app/templates/base.html only (lines ~398-653)
- Existing CSS at lines 243-261 can gain a \`.hover-preview.stub\` modifier for visual distinction
- Ties into wikihub-zmzk (broader stub styling)

title: Simplify account trigger to avatar-only in top nav
description: Remove the inline @username label from the authenticated account trigger in the top-right nav while keeping the username visible in the dropdown summary.
acceptance_criteria: Top nav account trigger shows avatar affordance without inline username text; dropdown still shows display name and @username.
close_reason: Removed inline username from top-nav account trigger, kept identity visible in dropdown, verified with full e2e suite, and pushed.

title: Group ACL grants: @group:name:role + groups membership model
description:
Extracted from wikihub-ibt.16 (v3 epic) as a standalone so it can land before the rest of ibt.

Today grants in `.wikihub/acl` are user-scoped (`pattern @username:role`). Users are asking for group-scoped grants for the standard reason — sharing to a named set of people (friends, team, family) without listing them all on every resource.

## Proposed surface

### Data model
New `Group` table: `{id, owner_id, name, created_at}`. New `GroupMembership`: `{group_id, user_id, added_at, added_by_user_id}`. Group names are per-owner (Jacob's "friends" ≠ Alice's "friends"), referenced in ACL as `@jacob/friends:role` or `@friends:role` in Jacob's own wiki (short form resolves to owner's namespace).

### ACL syntax extension
```
research/*       @jacob/friends:read
notes/public/*   @friends:edit
```
Most-specific wins, same as user grants. `resolve_grants` in app/acl.py walks group grants and expands to member users. Caching strategy TBD (probably "expand on read, invalidate on membership change").

### API
```
POST   /api/v1/groups                    { name, members: [] }
GET    /api/v1/groups                    (list own groups)
GET    /api/v1/groups/<name>             (members + grants using this group)
PATCH  /api/v1/groups/<name>             { add_members?, remove_members?, rename? }
DELETE /api/v1/groups/<name>             (fails if in use unless force)
POST   /api/v1/wikis/<o>/<s>/share       { pattern, group: "name", role }  # alongside existing username
```

### UI
Group management in /settings → "Groups" section. CRUD + member list. Share modal (wikihub-ebi's) grows a "Group" option alongside user autocomplete.

## Why standalone (not inside ibt.16)

wikihub-ibt.16 bundles groups + signed-in mode + link-share + link:token:role. Each is independently useful. Groups alone unlock team sharing, which is frequently-asked. Shipping it separately reduces risk and accelerates delivery.

## The two-axis principle this fits under

This is the **resource-ACL axis** (what principals a resource is shared with). Independent from the key-scope axis (what subset of the authenticating principal's access a given key activates — wikihub-gzlt). A scoped key that belongs to Jacob sees pages shared with `@jacob/friends` exactly as Jacob sees them — unless the key scope shrinks below that.

## Out of scope
- Groups with federated members across owners (e.g., a group that contains users from different accounts) — defer; v1 groups are owner-scoped
- Nested groups — defer
- Group chat as a first-class feature (different thing entirely)

## Dependencies
- wikihub-ibt.16 (parent v3 epic) — this ticket extracts the groups part to ship independently
- Pairs with wikihub-iga9 (bulk collaborator add), wikihub-skp7 (pending invites)

title: Explain Reindex action with hover help
description: Add concise hover help / tooltip copy for the Reindex action so users understand what it actually does before clicking it. Scope: explain what gets rebuilt, what it does not change, whether it is safe to run repeatedly, and where this explanation should live on desktop and mobile.

title: Per-line sharing: extend private bands with @user grants
description: Extend private bands (`<\!-- private -->`) to support per-user visibility: `<\!-- private @alice @bob -->`. Content inside these bands would be stripped for all users except those named. This is a natural extension of both the private band system and the ACL grant system — bands control inline visibility, ACL controls page/folder/wiki visibility. Implementation would need: (1) extend _scan_private_markers to parse usernames from opening tag, (2) extend strip_private_bands to accept a user parameter and only strip bands the user isn't granted, (3) update the renderer pipeline to pass current user through, (4) update public mirror generation to fully strip all user-scoped private bands. Deferred — build after page/folder/wiki-level sharing is solid.

title: link hover preview cards (LessWrong-style)
description: when hovering over a wikilink or internal link, show a popover card with a rendered snippet of the target page. cards should be approximately square, show the first ~paragraph of content with a (read more) link, and stack when multiple links are hovered in sequence (like LessWrong). should work for both [[wikilinks]] and regular markdown links to wiki pages. reference: LessWrong's hover preview system.

title: Profile vs personal wiki: clicking a person's name should go to /@username, not /@username/username
description:
On the Explore page, wiki cards for personal wikis (where slug == username) link to /@username/username (the wiki root), not /@username (the profile). This is confusing because:

1. The profile page IS the personal wiki rendered — they show the same content
2. But the profile has the user header, wiki sidebar with project wikis, and 'Wikis' section
3. The wiki root is just the wiki content without the profile chrome

For personal wikis, links should go to /@username (the profile) everywhere:
- Explore page wiki cards
- Search results
- Any place that shows a personal wiki as a card/link

For project wikis (slug != username), links should go to /@username/slug as normal.

The deeper question: should there even be a difference between /@username and /@username/username? Currently the profile page renders the personal wiki's index.md as its main content. Maybe /@username/username should just redirect to /@username. That would make the personal wiki's web identity always be the profile URL, while git clone still uses /@username/username.git.
close_reason: Personal wiki links go to profile. /@user/user redirects 302 to /@user.

title: Curate: Stanford Entrepreneurship resources
description:
Build a curated resource list for Stanford entrepreneurship. Candidates to include:

- StartX accelerator
- Stanford d.school
- BASES (student org) + BASES Challenge
